title: LASER-SCL: Learning-dynamics Aware Sample weighting with Expected-loss for Supervised Contrastive Learning
description: A trajectory-based sample weighting framework for supervised contrastive learning that uses Expected Learning Progress (ELP)—the trend of loss decrease over a sliding window—to distinguish learnable hard samples from noise. Unlike meta-learning approaches (Han et al., 2022; MW-Net) that require clean validation data, LASER-SCL computes weights directly from learning dynamics using predicted future loss for curriculum scheduling.
motivation: Supervised contrastive learning (SupCon) treats all samples uniformly, making it vulnerable to label noise. Existing solutions either require clean meta-data (MW-Net, CMW-Net), multiple networks (UNICON, Co-teaching), or meta-learning (Han et al., 2022). There is no method that provides adaptive sample-level weighting for supervised contrastive learning using only learning dynamics from a single network. Simple loss-based reweighting baselines have not been thoroughly compared against trajectory-based approaches in the SupCon context.
proposed_approach: LASER-SCL tracks each sample's loss trajectory over a sliding window and computes Expected Learning Progress (ELP)—the linear trend of loss decrease. It computes difficulty using predicted future loss (based on the fitted trajectory) rather than current loss, then applies a curriculum schedule that gradually shifts weight from easy to hard samples. No clean meta-data, meta-learning, or multiple networks are required.
key_innovations: Trajectory-based weighting: Uses ELP (loss trend) rather than just current loss or learned weights to distinguish learnable samples from noise
Predicted future loss curriculum: Schedules based on predicted future difficulty, not just current embedding similarity or static loss values
Meta-learning-free: Unlike Han et al. (2022), no auxiliary weight prediction network or meta-optimization is required
Single-network: Unlike UNICON, no co-teaching or multiple networks needed

related_work.title: Learning to Weight Samples for Dynamic Early-exiting Networks
related_work.key_difference: Han et al. learn a weight prediction network via meta-learning with pseudo-updates on meta-batches. LASER-SCL directly computes weights from loss trajectories without meta-learning or auxiliary networks.

related_work.title: UNICON: Combating Label Noise Through Uniform Selection and Contrastive Learning
related_work.key_difference: UNICON uses binary selection (clean vs noisy) with two-network co-teaching and Jensen-Shannon divergence. LASER-SCL uses continuous weighting with single-network learning dynamics.

related_work.title: Meta-Weight-Net: Learning an Explicit Mapping For Sample Weighting
related_work.key_difference: MW-Net requires clean meta-data for meta-learning. LASER-SCL eliminates this requirement entirely.

related_work.title: Mining negative samples on contrastive learning via curricular weighting strategy
related_work.key_difference: Zhuang et al. applies curriculum to self-supervised contrastive learning using embedding similarity. LASER-SCL operates in supervised setting with learning dynamics-based difficulty.

related_work.title: An Inclusive Theoretical Framework of Robust Supervised Contrastive Loss against Label Noise
related_work.key_difference: SymNCE modifies the loss function for robustness. LASER-SCL uses sample weighting, which is complementary and can be combined with robust losses.

hypothesis: A trajectory-based sample weighting mechanism using Expected Learning Progress (ELP) will more accurately distinguish learnable samples from noise than static loss-based reweighting in supervised contrastive learning, without requiring meta-learning, clean validation data, or multiple networks.

confirmation: LASER-SCL outperforms SupCon+Loss Reweighting by ≥2% under 40% label noise on CIFAR-100
Each component (curriculum, ELP) contributes ≥0.5% improvement when ablated
ELP-based selection achieves higher precision than loss-based selection (AUC-ROC)
Computational overhead <20% compared to vanilla SupCon
refutation: LASER-SCL performs worse than SupCon+LR across all noise conditions
Learning dynamics tracking provides no improvement over using just current loss
ELP scores show no correlation with true label correctness

datasets: CIFAR-10 with synthetic symmetric/asymmetric label noise
CIFAR-100 with synthetic symmetric/asymmetric label noise
scope_statement: This proposal focuses specifically on sample weighting for supervised contrastive learning under label noise. The key novelty is demonstrating that learning dynamics-based weighting (ELP) without meta-learning can outperform both meta-learning methods (Han et al., MW-Net) and simple loss-based reweighting. Class imbalance and hard negative mining are explicitly out of scope for this initial work.
